title: String enums
instruction:
String enums assign explicit string values to each member. They're more readable than numeric enums in logs and APIs.

Run this.
successMessage: String enums are the safest kind — they appear as readable strings in JSON and logs instead of opaque numbers.

title: Numeric enums
instruction:
Numeric enums auto-assign incrementing values starting at 0. They support reverse mapping — you can look up the name from the value.

Run this.
successMessage: `Priority[p]` is reverse mapping — numeric enums compile a lookup object so you can go both ways.

title: Enum in a function
instruction:
Use an enum as a parameter type to restrict what values can be passed.

Run this.
successMessage: The `Day` enum acts as a closed set — you can only pass valid enum members, not arbitrary strings.
